prompt: Refactor the specified code to improve its quality without changing behavior.
steps: Read and understand the current implementation thoroughly.
Identify the specific quality issues to address (complexity, duplication, naming, structure).
Plan the refactoring approach — ensure behavior is preserved.
Implement the refactoring in small, logical steps.
Run the existing test suite after each significant change to verify no regressions.
Run the linter and fix any violations.
Commit with a message that explains what was refactored and why.
extra_instructions: Do NOT change any public interfaces or behavior. If tests need updating because of internal restructuring, update them. Do not add new features or fix unrelated bugs during a refactor.
